w1.text:
# Week 1 Training Analysis

## 1. Weekly Summary

**What was trained:**
- 4 of 5 planned sessions completed (Lower Shape skipped entirely)
- Lower Power, Upper Strength, Run + Core, Upper Hypertrophy, partial Speed Conditioning
- Total high-intensity work: 2 lower body days, 2 upper body days, 1 conditioning session

**Volume highlights:**
- **Strong lifts:** Trap bar DL 140kg x 2x8 (RPE 9), Barbell row 80kg (RPE 8)
- **Decent upper work:** DB bench 68kg, Weighted pull-ups +10kg x 3 sets
- **Conditioning:** Pushed hard on intervals (10 rounds instead of 8, RPE 9)
- **Incomplete sessions:** Upper Hypertrophy severely truncated (only 1-2 sets per exercise), core work skipped, speed work mostly skipped

**Red flags:**
- Multiple incomplete workouts suggest fatigue or time constraints
- Upper Hypertrophy session particularly affected (noted 750m farmer carries earlier same day)
- Split squat instability issue noted

## 2. Progressive Overload Recommendations

### Lower Power (Week 2):
- **Trap bar DL:** Drop to **135kg for 3x8** (RPE 9 for 2 sets is too aggressive this early; add a set, reduce weight slightly)
- **Split squat:** Stay at **22kg but use flat surface**, focus on stability, 3x8 per leg
- **Box/Squat jump:** Increase to **3x6 reps** at same height
- **Nordic curls:** Continue bodyweight, aim for **3x4-5 controlled reps**
- **Calf raise:** Increase to **85kg single-leg** on leg press, 3x12

### Upper Strength (Week 2):
- **Bench press (DB):** Increase to **70kg (35kg DBs), 3x6-8** (RPE 8 is perfect training zone)
- **Weighted pull-up:** Increase to **12.5kg, 3x5-6 reps** (RPE 6 means plenty in reserve)
- **Barbell row:** Increase to **82.5kg, 3x8** (solid RPE 8)
- **Face pull/Rear delt:** Maintain **32kg, add 1 set** (3x12-15)

### Run + Core (Week 2):
- **Intervals:** Return to prescribed **8 rounds of 30s/90s rest** (you did 10x30s/60s at RPE 9—too much)
- **Farmer carry:** Increase to **32kg per hand, 3x30-40m**
- **COMPLETE THE CORE WORK:** Side plank 3x30s each side, Dead bug 3x10

### Upper Hypertrophy (Week 2):
- **DO NOT schedule heavy carries same day**
- **Incline DB press:** **70kg for 3x10-12** (you only did 1 set at 80kg/RPE 9)
- **Lat pulldown:** **77.5kg for 3x8-10** (reduce slightly to complete full volume)
- **Lateral raise:** **12kg for 3x12-15** (complete all sets)
- **EZ curl:** **22.5kg for 3x10-12**
- **OH tricep ext:** **32kg DB for 3x10-12**
- **Rear delt row:** **25kg for 3x12** (30kg at RPE 10 for only 5 reps is too heavy)

### Speed Conditioning (Week 2):
- **COMPLETE THIS SESSION**
- **Ankle mobility:** 2x10 circles each direction + 2x30s calf stretch
- **Tib raises:** 3x15-20 bodyweight
- **Add:** 2-3 sets of low pogo jumps or ankle bounces (low intensity ankle plyos)

## 3. Ankle Recovery Notes

**Ankle feel: 4/5** — This is solid, but there are concerns

w2.text:
# WEEK 2 TRAINING ANALYSIS

## 1. WEEKLY SUMMARY

**What Was Trained:**
- 4 training sessions: Lower Power, Upper Strength, Lower Shape, Speed/Power
- 1 Zone 2 cardiovascular session
- Plyometric work (pogos, verticals, depth drops)
- Main strength lifts (trap bar DL, bench, rows, pull-ups)
- Unilateral lower body work (split squats, Bulgarian split squats, SL RDL)
- Brief sprint work

**Volume Highlights:**
- **Massive jump in pogo hops:** 60 total reps (up from 24 prev week - 150% increase)
- **Trap bar DL introduced:** 110 kg for 8, 7 reps (solid debut)
- **Bench press progressed:** 72 kg x 7, 6 (up from 68 kg)
- **Weighted pull-ups advanced:** +12 kg x 6, 5, 5 (up from +10 kg)
- **Incomplete sets noted:** Several exercises have missing rep counts (vertical jumps, Bulgarian split squats, lateral raises)

**Overall:** Solid week with good strength progressions. Data tracking needs tightening.

---

## 2. PROGRESSIVE OVERLOAD RECOMMENDATIONS

### Lower Power
- **Pogo hops:** Hold at 5×5 (75 total). Last week's jump was aggressive; consolidate before progressing
- **Vertical jump:** Complete 2×6 next week at 140 cm box (finish what you started)
- **Depth drop:** 22 cm - aim for 3×6 reps (you did 7, 5 this week; add consistency)
- **Trap bar DL:** **Go to 115 kg** for 2×7 reps (strong first showing at 110)
- **Split squat/Calf machine:** Clarify exercise selection. If calf raises: log weight properly and aim for 2×12

### Upper Strength
- **Bench press:** **Go to 75 kg** for 2×6+ reps (hit 7, 6 at 72 kg - clear progression signal)
- **Weighted pull-up:** **Stay at +12 kg**, aim for 6, 6, 5 (add 1 rep to set 2)
- **Barbell row:** **Go to 87.5 kg** for 2×5 reps (solid 2×5 at 85 kg)
- **Lateral raise:** 14 kg - complete 2×8 with ALL reps recorded
- **Preacher curls:** Stay at 32.5 kg, aim for 15, 12 (build set 2)
- **Tricep pushdown:** **Go to 38 kg** for 8, 6 reps

### Lower Shape
- **Single leg RDL:** **Go to 32 kg** for 2×6 reps (hit 6, 4 this week)
- **Bulgarian split squat:** 24 kg - complete 2×8 reps BOTH SIDES, record all reps

### Speed + Power
- **Sprints:** Progress to **4-5 × 15-20s** with full recovery (you only logged 3 min total - unclear volume)

---

## 3. ANKLE RECOVERY NOTES

**Ankle Feel: 5/5** ✅

**Assessment:** Excellent score. Significant plyometric volume increase (pogo hops +150%) was well-tolerated.

**Recommendations:**
- Continue current loading pattern - ankle is responding well
- Monitor response to sustained pogo volume (you increased aggressively; watch for delayed soreness)
- The conservative split squat/unilateral work is appropriate - maintain this
- Depth drops at 22 cm are safe; don't rush to increase height
- Easy run at 5:51/km pace was appropriate (RPE 6) - keep zone 2 work easy

**Green light to progress, but hold plyometric volume steady for one more week before next jump.**

---

## 4. TOP 3 FOCUS POINTS FOR NEXT WEEK

### **#1

wed.note: Hinge at hip, soft knee, weight close to leg, feel hamstring load. Light DB to start — balance is the challenge not weight. Trains gait stability and hip control.
Rear foot elevated. Knee tracks over toes.
Squeeze glutes at top, tuck pelvis, full hip extension.
Full range — bottom stretch to tip-toe top.

mon.note: Tuck elbows if neck sensitive. Controlled descent.
Or park bar bodyweight. Full hang at bottom.
Chest-supported if available. Drive elbows back.

sun.note: Stay on balls of feet, minimal ground contact time, small quick hops. Trains ankle stiffness and reactive power.
Max height, full reset between reps
Step off a low step or kerb. Focus entirely on soft quiet landing — absorb through ankle, knee, hip. This is landing mechanics training.
Hinge focus. Hip hinge not squat.
Front foot on plate or step. Control descent.
Single-leg right side. Full range both directions.

mon.note: Tuck elbows if neck sensitive. Controlled descent.
Or park bar bodyweight. Full hang at bottom.
Chest-supported if available. Drive elbows back.

tue.note: Conversational pace — you should be able to hold a sentence. Nasal breathing preferred.
Stack feet or stagger. Keep hips high.
Press lower back into floor the entire time. Move slow.
Arms overhead, legs low, lower back pressed down.

wed.note: Hinge at hip, soft knee, weight close to leg, feel hamstring load. Light DB to start — balance is the challenge not weight. Trains gait stability and hip control.
Rear foot elevated. Knee tracks over toes.
Squeeze glutes at top, tuck pelvis, full hip extension.
Full range — bottom stretch to tip-toe top.

thu.note: Wide grip. Pull bar to upper chest, lean back slightly.
Slight forward lean, lead with elbow.

fri.note: 40–60% effort — controlled, not maximal. 2 min walk between. Focus on mechanics.
Add from week 4-5 only — when ankle feel is 4+ for two consecutive weeks. Use bench or low wall for box jumps.
Slow circles both directions. Deep calf stretch with straight and bent knee.
Heels on edge, pull toes up to full range.
Press lower back and head into wall throughout. Elbows and wrists stay on wall.
